Chrome setup guide

How to Block Ads on Chrome (2026 Guide)

Google Chrome doesn't have a built-in ad blocker, but adding one takes less than 60 seconds. This guide shows you how to block ads on Chrome using AdShield Pro — a free, Manifest V3-compatible extension.

Step 1 — Open the Chrome Web Store

Go to the AdShield Pro Chrome Web Store page or search 'AdShield Pro' in the Chrome Web Store.

AdShield Pro Web Store
Step 2 — Click 'Add to Chrome'

Click the blue 'Add to Chrome' button. A confirmation dialog will appear — click 'Add extension.'

If you need help, see Support.

Step 3 — Activate Ad Blocking

AdShield Pro starts blocking ads immediately after installation. You'll see the shield icon in your Chrome toolbar — it's already working.

Learn more about controls on Features.

Step 4 — Customize (Optional)

Click the AdShield Pro icon to toggle blocking on/off, whitelist specific sites, or upgrade to Pro for advanced filtering controls.

Read the Privacy.

What Gets Blocked?

Once installed, AdShield Pro automatically blocks banner ads, pop-ups, tracking scripts, and ad network requests across websites. Does it work on YouTube? Ad blocking effectiveness on streaming platforms like YouTube varies as these platforms frequently update their ad delivery methods. AdShield Pro blocks many ad types, though some video ads may still appear. How to Stop Trackers or the overview page Tracker Blocker.

Is It Safe to Use an Ad Blocker on Chrome?

Yes. AdShield Pro does not access your personal data, passwords, or browsing history. All filtering is done locally using Chrome's native API.

Add AdShield Pro to Chrome — Free → See pricing
